title: Sunday Pot Roast
description: A tender, slow-cooked pot roast with carrots and potatoes — a Pryor family Sunday tradition.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 325°F. Pat roast dry and season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear roast on all sides until deep brown, about 4 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add onion and garlic; cook 3 minutes until softened. Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 minute.
Pour in wine and broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Add thyme and rosemary.
Return roast to the pot. Add carrots and potatoes around the roast. Liquid should come about halfway up the roast.
Cover and transfer to oven. Braise for 3.5–4 hours, until the meat is fork-tender and falling apart.
Rest 10 minutes before serving. Taste the braising liquid and season with salt and pepper; serve as the gravy.
